paper_title: Microbial and Hydrochemical Analysis of Proschalnaya Cave

methods.description: Quantitative PCR (qPCR) analysis was used to investigate microbial presence in moonmilk samples, determining the total number of eubacterial DNA copies and specific bacteria genera using specialized primers.

steps.description: DNA was extracted using a GeneMATRIX Soil DNA Purification Kit.
steps.searchable_summary: extract DNA from samples using GeneMATRIX Soil DNA Purification Kit

steps.description: The total number of eubacterial DNA copies and the DNA copies of bacteria of the genera Rhodoferax and Geothrix were determined with use of specialized primers.
steps.searchable_summary: determine DNA copies using specialized primers

steps.description: Quantitative PCR was performed with an RG-6000-5 Plex real-time DNA cycler.
steps.searchable_summary: perform qPCR using RG-6000-5 Plex real-time DNA cycler

steps.description: Non-DNA-containing samples were used as negative controls to ensure the accuracy of the qPCR.
steps.searchable_summary: use negative controls to validate qPCR accuracy

steps.description: Cycles of qPCR characterized: 40 cycles for Eubacteria, 40 cycles for Rhodoferax ferrireducens, 45 cycles for Geothrix fermentans.
steps.searchable_summary: characterize qPCR cycles for different bacteria

steps.description: Quantification was performed using standard curves obtained from the amplification profiles of known concentrations of the respective standard.
A melt curve analysis (55-99 °C) was performed at the end of PCR cycles to confirm specificity of primer annealing.
steps.searchable_summary: perform melt curve analysis to confirm primer specificity

methods.description: Characterization of water and moonmilk samples collected from Proschalnaya Cave and Sagdi-Selanka River, including elemental composition analysis.

steps.description: Water samples of different origins were taken from the cave and the Sagdi-Selanka River according to the standards of sampling in hydrochemistry and microbiology.
steps.searchable_summary: collect water samples from cave and river

steps.description: Samples of moonmilk deposits of different consistencies were aseptically collected in sterile tubes.
Samples were transported to the laboratory in coolers at 4 °C.
Determination of the elemental composition in samples of natural waters and moonmilk was carried out with use of the Total Quant ICP-MS method.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze elemental composition using Total Quant ICP-MS
